hacking.notes: Initial access vector not disclosed; label Hacking-ext-unknown.

social.notes: ShinyHunters posted an extortion/leak threat on their dark web leak site demanding Kodak make contact by 18 June 2026 before publishing the stolen data. Kodak declined to pay.

plus.analyst_notes:
Closes https://github.com/vz-risk/VCDB/issues/23383
Encoded by AI — encode-veris-incident skill version 20260702T132320Z

Eastman Kodak Company (imaging/photographic materials manufacturer, HQ Rochester NY) confirmed in June 2026 that an unauthorized third party gained temporary access to a limited amount of company data. The ShinyHunters extortion gang claimed responsibility on their dark-web leak site (listed ~15 June 2026), claiming over 2.2 million records of customer PII and internal corporate data, and threatened to leak the data if Kodak did not make contact by 18 June 2026. Kodak declined to pay, engaged external cybersecurity experts, and notified law enforcement.

Attribution: ShinyHunters self-claimed the breach on their leak site; Kodak confirmed a breach but had not independently attributed or verified the full scope at time of reporting. Coded as external Organized crime / Financial per the group's established financially-motivated data-extortion MO (breach -> exfiltrate -> ransom demand -> leak/auction).

Attacker-sourced / not victim-confirmed details (noted per skill): the 2,200,000 record count and the exact data categories are ShinyHunters' claims; Kodak characterized the accessed data as 'limited' and the group provided no public proof. data_total set to the attacker-claimed 2.2M with this caveat. Vector/variety of the intrusion is undisclosed (label Hacking-ext-unknown), coded Unknown; hosting unknown so asset.cloud = Unknown (ShinyHunters' broader 2026 campaign targeted cloud SaaS platforms, but the Kodak-specific vector is unconfirmed).

Extortion coded under action.social (documented leak/publish threat + deadline); required Alter behavior integrity impact and a People asset added. Extortion demand was refused. discovery_method = Actor disclosure (attacker publicized it via leak site). data_abuse = 'Yes - Data ransomed' (extortion/leak-site component).

Timeline: initial-access date not disclosed by sources; incident year coded 2026 (activity/disclosure all June 2026). dbir_year derived as 2027 (incident/disclosure in Jan-Oct 2026 window).

Unknown/low-confidence fields: hacking variety & vector; asset hosting (cloud); actor country; social target & precise vector; initial-access date; whether the data was ultimately published after the deadline (no source confirms publication as of 2026-07-02).

Sources consulted: BleepingComputer (issue-linked); Security Boulevard, computing.co.uk, Security Magazine, teiss (issue-linked comments); Malwarebytes (2026-06-18) and Cybernews (independent, corroborating Kodak's refusal to pay and lack of public proof) found via web search. Newer sources did not contradict earlier reporting; they add that Kodak declined the ransom and no leak had been publicly confirmed.

Reviewed by swidup 2026-07-10: changed event_chain step 1 attribute from ia to unk since the entry vector — and therefore the attribute — is undisclosed.

event_chain.summary: Initial access to Kodak data; method undisclosed
Customer PII and internal corporate data exfiltrated
Extortion / leak threat with 18 June 2026 deadline

summary: Eastman Kodak Company confirmed a June 2026 data breach after the ShinyHunters extortion gang claimed on its dark-web leak site to have stolen over 2.2 million records of customer PII and internal corporate data, threatening to publish the data unless Kodak made contact by 18 June 2026. Kodak confirmed an unauthorized third party gained temporary access to a limited amount of its data, declined to pay, and engaged external cybersecurity experts and law enforcement.
